zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Overman於1798年出生在美國北卡羅來納州時，她的父親Charles Overman38歲，母親Mary Ann Scarborough33歲。她於1819年2月4日與Noah Cartwright在美國印第安納州克拉克福森維爾 堂史普福森維爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1849年在美國印第安納州克拉克享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Overman于1798年出生在美国北卡罗来纳州时，她的父亲Charles Overman38岁，母亲Mary Ann Scarborough33岁。她于1819年2月4日与Noah Cartwright在美国印第安纳州克拉克福森維爾 堂史普福森維爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1849年在美国印第安纳州克拉克享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Overman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: status name from Middle Dutch overman ‘judge, arbitrator, go-between’.
English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English overman ‘leader, ruler; supervisor, overseer’, perhaps also ‘judge, arbitrator’ (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Overland
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Borge, Erik, Jan Erik, Nordahl, Tryg.

Norwegian (mainly Øverland): habitational name from any of some twenty farmsteads, mainly in Telemark and on the west coast, named Øverland, Overland, from øver, over ‘upper’ + land ‘(piece of) land, farmstead'.

English (Norfolk and Cambridgeshire): apparently a habitational name from Overland Farm in Ash next Sandwich (Kent), although the name is now strongly associated with northern East Anglia. The placename derives from Old English ōfer ‘bank’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Overmann
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived on the bank of a river, from Middle Low German over ‘river bank’ + man ‘man’.
status name for a judge or arbitrator (compare Dutch   Overman  ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Gold Nuggets Found
historicEvents.description: "In 1799, in Little Meadow Creak located in Cabarrus County, North Carolina a large yellow """"rock"""" was found by Conrad Reed. A few years later it was determined that the """"rock"""" was a gold nugget."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Legends of America- Reed Gold Mine, North Carolina- First Gold Discovery

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

historicEvents.title: War of 1812
historicEvents.description: Because of the outbreak of war from Napoleonic France, Britain decided to blockade the trade between the United States and the French. The US then fought this action and said it was illegal under international law. Britain supplied Native Americans who raided settlers living on the frontier and halting expansion westward. In 1814, one of the British raids stormed into Washington D.C. burning down the capital. Neither the Americans or the British wanted to continue fighting, so negotiations of peace began. After Treaty of Ghent was signed, Unaware of the treaty, British forces invaded Louisiana but were defeated in January 1815.

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: Trail of Tears
historicEvents.description: In the 1830's, President Jackson called for all the Native Americans to be forced off their own land. As the Cherokee were forced out of North Carolina many of them hid in the mountains of North Carolina.
historicEvents.urlTitle: North Carolina Trail of Tears Association

historicEvents.title: Indiana Nears Bankruptcy
historicEvents.description: The State of Indiana was near bankruptcy in 1841 due to the inability to repay interest incurred for the Massive Internal Improvement Act. The state liquidated much of its public works. Many of the projects were handed over to the state’s creditors as a way to reduce debt. Only two of the eight proposed infrastructure projects were completed by the creditors.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Overman was born in 1798, in North Carolina, United States, her father, Charles Overman, was 38 and her mother, Mary Ann Scarborough, was 33. She married Noah Cartwright on 4 February 1819, in Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died about 1849, in Clark, Indiana, United States, at the age of 52.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Overmanが1798年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州で生まれた時，父Charles Overmanは38歳，母Mary Ann Scarboroughは33歳であった。1819年2月4日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークジェフエルソンヴィル トウンシュイップジェフエルソンヴィルでNoah Cartwrightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1849年にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークで52歳で死去した。

ko.Jeffersonville, Clark, Indiana: 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크 제퍼손빌 타운시프 제퍼손빌
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Overman(이)가 1798년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Charles Overman(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Ann Scarborough(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1819년 2월 4일 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크 제퍼손빌 타운시프 제퍼손빌에서 Noah Cartwright(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1849년 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Jeffersonville, Clark, Indiana: джеффирсонвилл, джеффирсонвилл тоуншип, кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Overman родилась в 1798 г. в местности Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Charles Overman было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Ann Scarborough, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Noah Cartwright 4 февраля 1819 г. в местности джеффирсонвилл, джеффирсонвилл тоуншип, кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла около 1849 г. в местности кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 52 лет.
